findings.detail: Side-by-side two-column layout exactly matches intent: left panel (Full Sample Space) shows all 6 die outcomes as a starting node with event A (even rolls {2,4,6}) highlighted in hitl-yellow below it; right panel (Reduced Sample Space) shows condition B applied (die > 3, auto-purple), remaining outcomes reduced to {4,5,6}, event A within B re-highlighted in hitl-yellow ({4,6}), and the final P(A|B) = 2/3 result in done-green. Columns are height-balanced at 2 and 3 nodes respectively — minor asymmetry acceptable given the right panel carries one extra result node.

No out-of-scope concepts: 'Bayes theorem', 'joint probability', 'independence', 'posterior' all absent. Every label traces directly to corpus §5.3 (reduced sample space) and §5.4 (P(A|B) formula). Color tier: entry nodes use start-blue, event-A highlight nodes use hitl-yellow (correctly — these are the 'human-identifiable' outcomes the student must spot), condition node uses auto-purple, result uses done-green.

stats.notes: Source-level pre-flight complete. PNG requires mmdc CLI render: mmdc -i diagram.mmd -o diagram.png -w 1400 -b transparent. All source-level checks pass; visual inspection pending PNG generation.
